input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T00:48:30.826Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup, and also invoke the relevant skill for MTP migration.

toolResult.sessionLog:

--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158
@@ -1,0 +1,2 @@
 ConditionalTests.cs

result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158 b/tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158
@@ -1,0 +1,2 @@
 ConditionalTests.cs
 TestProject.csproj

data.content:
**Exit code 8** is MTP's "no tests discovered" code. VSTest returned 0 in that scenario; MTP treats it as an error by default.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered,
         e.g. when conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), exit code 8 means "no tests were found." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, all tests are compiled out, so MTP returns exit code 8, which fails CI.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` in the project file (or `Directory.Build.props`). This tells MTP to return exit code 0 instead of 8 when no tests are discovered.

Let me apply this fix.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered,
         e.g. when conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (instead of 8) when no tests are discovered,
         e.g. when conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.content: Now let me verify the fix works — building and running **without** `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` should return exit code 0:

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158 && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Run tests without conditional flag", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content: Exit code is now **0** with zero tests. Let me also confirm tests still run and pass when the flag is defined:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with conditional flag enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with conditional flag enabled

arguments.description: Run tests with conditional flag enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-0f877d681e3d4ecfa5052271abf34158 && dotnet test -p:DefineConstants=INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Run tests with conditional flag enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with conditional flag enabled

data.content:
**Summary:**

MTP exit code 8 = "no tests discovered." VSTest returned 0 in that case; MTP intentionally flags it as an error.

The fix is one property:

```xml
<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
```

**Verified:** zero-test builds now exit 0, and actual test runs still pass normally. If you have multiple test projects, consider placing this in a `Directory.Build.props` so it applies everywhere.
